name: validate-client-intelligence-packet
description: Accept a real Merraine intelligence packet and reject an unsafe incomplete packet.
given: The opulent-gtm-intelligence skill validator and packets containing live-client evidence identifiers.
when: The validator runs against a complete packet and a packet with invalid dates, scores, confidence, evidence, contact provenance, and verification.
then: The complete packet exits zero and the unsafe packet exits non-zero with blocking issues.

name: render-merraine-relationship-intelligence
description: Render a client-ready relationship intelligence overview and dossiers from publicly evidenced Merraine engagements.
given: A real Merraine packet using Mayberg Foundation, ACSZ, and Oak Valley Hospital District public evidence.
when: The packet is validated and rendered through the bundled HTML templates.
then: The overview and three dossiers exist, contain relationship paths and risks, contain no unresolved tokens, and preserve public-evidence labels.

name: govern-scheduled-gtm-applications-and-crm-updates
description: Accept a bounded proposed GTM application and reject an unsafe autonomous CRM mutation.
given: A real Merraine intelligence packet with proposed schedule and calendar applications plus an intentionally unsafe packet that autonomously changes a protected CRM stage.
when: Both packets run through the validator and the safe packet is rendered.
then: The safe packet passes and displays application contracts and CRM field diffs; the unsafe packet fails for missing schedule, budget, stop, receipt, confidence, and protected-field controls.

name: prioritize-recent-change-over-static-enrichment
description: Require every routed signal to prove and score a dated before/after change, then render the conversation implication above static context.
given: A public-evidence Merraine packet with one recent search mandate, one expired mandate, signal score components, relationship context, verification tasks, routes, and conversation angles.
when: The validator checks the signal contract and the renderer builds the overview and target dossiers.
then: The packet passes only when score components add up and required delta fields exist; the overview opens with What changed since last touch and each dossier embeds its target-specific signal ledger.

name: execute-context-top-of-funnel-intelligence
description: Require every Context.dev capability to pair an operator-readable natural-language job with an exact, auditable API contract.
given: A real public-domain Merraine monitor proposal with method, endpoint, params, semantic extraction body, request tags, expected response, Opulent routing, write policy, evidence, and proposed status, plus an intentionally vague and unreceipted executed operation.
when: Both packets run through the validator and the valid packet renders through the bundled dossier.
then: The complete operation passes and renders its natural-language job, API contract, parameters, policy, status, and receipt state; the vague operation fails for missing language, wrong method, malformed params, missing tags, and missing execution receipt.

name: route-person-discovery-by-intake-scope
description: Use a precision route for one person, a deduplicated shared-company route for supplied cohorts, and a privacy-preserving bounded route for calendar-derived cohorts.
given: A public-evidence single-person Merraine packet, a three-target supplied cohort, an intentionally unsafe calendar-derived scope, and duplicate company operations for one canonical domain.
when: The scope contracts and Context ledgers run through the real packet validator and the cohort renders through the intelligence template.
then: Single-person and supplied-list scopes pass with unique-identity Context ceilings; the rendered brief shows funnel counts and call budgets; the calendar scope fails for unbounded time, overspending, missing reuse, one-time monitors, unsafe attendee policy, and calendar payload transmission; duplicate company calls fail both canonical-reuse and budget checks.
